codeReview.summary: Reviewed the fix commit, handoff, and transcript skeleton against the prior blocking findings. The implementation closes the identified gaps: semantic graph projections now preserve handle identity, semantic decode errors carry the real section index, semantic decoding activates when records are prefixed/padded before the first semantic token, and parser-facing entity summaries preserve owner plus layer provenance including handle/reference-based layer relationships.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the DWG parser worker's verification-first option appropriately because the feature was already present in the shared worktree, but startup guidance still lacks a Windows-safe way to run `.factory/init.sh`. Both the handoff and transcript show failed attempts through `python3` and PowerShell before proceeding without it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff for session 3082e695-6be1-46f9-9f1e-345ea8417af8 marks `followedProcedure` false only for init-script execution and verification-first audit; `.factory/skills/dwg-parser-worker/SKILL.md` requires initialization but does not document a Windows invocation for `.factory/init.sh`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The semantic token conventions used by parser-facing provenance extraction are still only encoded in source/tests rather than shared library guidance. Future workers would have to rediscover that prefixed semantic detection scans for `TBL:/ENT:/OBJ:` anywhere in the payload and that entity provenance combines `LR<hex>`, `L<name>`, and `O<hex>` fragments into outward links.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `crates/h7cad-native-dwg/src/lib.rs:188-197,405-491` implements semantic prefix scanning and owner/layer link assembly, while `.factory/library/architecture.md` describes semantic provenance goals but not the current token conventions or activation rules.

summary: Reviewed feature `dwg-parser-semantic-extraction-scrutiny-fix` against the prior semantic-decoder and pending-layer/entity blocking findings. Commit `f07337e98fa1fa251a5f9694d15cc7a13aefb3af` resolves those issues, and I found no remaining blocking or non-blocking code defects in the reviewed feature scope.
